name: Agent / LLM-orchestration system
summary: A system in which one or more LLM-driven agents autonomously plan and take multi-step actions using tools/APIs to accomplish goals, choosing which actions to invoke based on model output. Its risk profile is dominated by the blast radius of delegated actions, non-determinism, and susceptibility to prompt injection.

concerns: direct and indirect prompt injection via tool outputs, retrieved documents, and web content (OWASP LLM01)
excessive agency (OWASP LLM06): least-privilege scoping of tools, permissions, and reachable systems
tool/action allow-listing and per-tool authorization; preventing confused-deputy and privilege escalation through tools
secrets and credential handling for tools; isolation so a compromised step cannot exfiltrate credentials or data
sandboxing of code-execution / shell / browser tools and egress control on agent-initiated network calls
questions: Is each tool scoped to least privilege, with an allow-list and per-action authorization?
Are direct and indirect prompt-injection defenses required across all untrusted content the agent ingests?
Are code-execution/browser tools sandboxed with controlled network egress, and are credentials isolated from model context?

concerns: blast radius of autonomous actions: which actions are irreversible, destructive, or high-consequence
human-in-the-loop approval gates for consequential/irreversible actions (spend, deletion, external comms, code merge)
guardrails and policy enforcement independent of the model (deterministic checks, not just prompt instructions)
safe-default and refusal behaviour for out-of-policy or ambiguous requests
containment of harmful downstream effects when the agent is wrong or manipulated
questions: Are high-consequence/irreversible actions gated behind human approval or dry-run confirmation?
Are guardrails enforced deterministically outside the model, not only via prompt instructions?
Is the blast radius of each tool classified, and is a safe default defined for ambiguous requests?

concerns: error and loop containment: max-step/iteration caps, recursion limits, and detection of unproductive loops
timeouts, retries with backoff, and idempotency of side-effecting tool calls to avoid duplicate actions
graceful handling of tool failures, malformed tool output, and partial progress
state/checkpoint management for long-running or resumable agent runs
deadlock/livelock avoidance in multi-agent coordination
questions: Are max-step/iteration caps and loop detection required to bound runaway execution?
Are side-effecting tool calls idempotent, with defined timeout/retry behaviour?
Is behaviour on tool failure or malformed tool output specified rather than left to the model?

concerns: goal/task specification: what the agent is allowed to attempt and what is explicitly out of scope
correctness of planning and tool selection; verification that the intended outcome was actually achieved
outcome evaluation against acceptance criteria (task success, not just plausible-looking output)
grounding/retrieval accuracy and hallucination control for factual tasks
clarification behaviour when the goal is under-specified
questions: Is task success defined by verifiable outcome criteria rather than plausible output?
Is the agent's allowed scope (and explicit out-of-scope) specified?
Is grounding/hallucination control required for factual or high-stakes tasks?

concerns: observability of agent decisions: full traces of prompts, tool calls, arguments, outputs, and reasoning steps
reproducibility/determinism controls (fixed seeds/temperature, versioned prompts and tool schemas) for debugging
an evaluation harness / regression suite over representative tasks with scored outcomes
prompt and agent-config versioning and change management
replay/debugging of a past run from its trace
questions: Is every agent run fully traceable (prompts, tool calls, arguments, outputs) for audit and debugging?
Is there an evaluation harness with scored task outcomes to catch regressions?
Are prompts and tool schemas versioned, with runs replayable from their traces?

concerns: monitoring of agent runs: success rate, cost, latency, action counts, and intervention rate
a kill switch / emergency stop to halt agents and revoke tool access in flight
rate limiting and concurrency control on agent-initiated actions against external systems
alerting on anomalous behaviour (cost spikes, action loops, repeated failures, policy hits)
runbooks for stuck, looping, or misbehaving agents
questions: Is there a kill switch to stop agents and revoke tool access mid-run?
Are success rate, cost, and intervention rate monitored with anomaly alerting?
Are rate limits on agent-initiated external actions defined?

concerns: token and cost budgets per task/run and enforcement when exceeded
end-to-end task latency across multi-step tool chains and model calls
context-window management, truncation, and summarisation strategy for long runs
parallelism of independent tool calls / sub-agents vs cost
questions: Are per-run token/cost budgets defined with enforced cutoffs?
Is an end-to-end task-latency target set across the multi-step chain?
Is context-window/truncation strategy specified for long-running tasks?

concerns: data exposure to third-party models and tools; what context/PII may leave the trust boundary
data-residency and retention of prompts, traces, and tool outputs that may contain personal data
accountability and attribution for actions the agent takes on a user's behalf
compliance of automated actions (consent, record-keeping) and applicable AI-regulation classification
questions: Is it specified what data (incl. PII) may be sent to external models/tools and retained in traces?
Is accountability/attribution defined for actions the agent performs on a user's behalf?
Is the applicable AI-regulation risk tier for autonomous actions addressed?

concerns: appropriate user trust and transparency: making the agent's actions and limits visible, avoiding over-reliance
task-completion effectiveness and how much human effort/oversight the outcome actually requires
user control: ability to steer, pause, correct, and undo agent actions
satisfaction and perceived reliability over repeated use
questions: Are the agent's actions and limits made transparent to keep user trust calibrated?
Can the user steer, pause, correct, or undo the agent's actions?
Is task-completion effectiveness (with acceptable oversight effort) measured?
